question_text: What happens to light when it enters a denser material, such as glass?

options.label: A
options.text: It speeds up and bends away from the normal

options.label: B
options.text: It slows down and bends towards the normal

options.label: C
options.text: It speeds up and bends towards the normal

options.label: D
options.text: It slows down and bends away from the normal

correct_answer: It slows down and bends towards the normal
explanation: When light travels from a less dense material (like air) into a denser material (like glass), it slows down because the material has more particles to interact with. As it slows down, it bends towards the normal line (the imaginary line perpendicular to the surface). This bending is called refraction.
